message:
Add gcs-backend/ — Parquet-on-GCS BackendAdapter (ADR-155 §M2)

The first cloud backend. Reads vector columns from Parquet files on
Google Cloud Storage; cache coherence rides the existing GCS
per-object generation token (every reupload bumps the gen → the
witness changes → ruLake invalidates the cache, all via the
existing Generation::Num variant in src/bundle.rs).

Auth is Application Default Credentials — no bespoke auth code; an
operator runs `gcloud auth application-default login` or sets
GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS and the object_store crate handles
the rest.

Parquet schema contract (v0.1):
- id     INT64           non-null  (cast to u64 by bit pattern)
- vector LIST<FLOAT32>   non-null  (or FixedSizeList<FLOAT32, N>)

Cheap current_bundle() override — required by ADR-004 §Resources for
the rulake://bundle/{backend}/{collection} resource. Does a HEAD on
the object (~1 RTT) + a footer-only schema read (a few KiB) instead
of the default impl's full pull_vectors. The default impl would melt
a remote backend at resource-read rates.

Sibling crate per ADR-001 (no root workspace; depends on
ruvector-rulake via path = "..").

Verification:
- cargo build --release  → silent
- cargo test --release   → 4/4 offline tests pass (in-memory ObjectStore)
  - pull_vectors_round_trip_offline
  - current_bundle_does_not_pull_vectors_offline
  - generation_changes_on_reupload_offline
  - unknown_collection_errors_with_typed_variant
- live test scaffold (gated on RULAKE_GCS_LIVE_TEST=1) ready for
  operator to run against their own bucket; #[ignore]'d by default.

Stack: object_store 0.13 (gcp feature), parquet 58 (object_store +
async features), arrow 58, single-thread tokio runtime owned by the
backend so the sync BackendAdapter trait stays satisfied.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+# ruLake GCS backend — Parquet-on-GCS BackendAdapter (ADR-155 §M2).
+#
+# Implements `BackendAdapter` for Parquet vector files stored on
+# Google Cloud Storage. Generation = GCS object generation number
+# (`Generation::Num`) so cache coherence rides the existing GCS
+# atomicity guarantees — every successful upload bumps the gen.
+#
+# Standalone Cargo package per ADR-001 (no root workspace).
+#
+# Build:  cargo build --release
+# Test:   cargo test --release         (offline; smoke against fixtures)
+#         RULAKE_GCS_LIVE_TEST=1 \
+#         RULAKE_GCS_BUCKET=ruv-rulake-test cargo test --release -- --ignored
+#                                       (live; needs gcloud ADC)
+
+[package]
+name = "ruvector-rulake-gcs"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2021"
+license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
+authors = ["Ruvector Team"]
+repository = "https://github.com/ruvnet/RuLake"
+description = "GCS Parquet BackendAdapter for ruLake (ADR-155 §M2)"
+
+[dependencies]
+ruvector-rulake = { path = ".." }
+
+# Unified storage abstraction. The `gcp` feature pulls in the GCS
+# transport (which honours gcloud Application Default Credentials).
+# `tokio` feature lets us use the async API; we wrap it in a blocking
+# bridge so the BackendAdapter trait (sync) stays satisfied.
+object_store = { version = "0.13", default-features = false, features = ["gcp"] }
+
+# Apache Parquet reader. `arrow` feature gets us the RecordBatch API
+# we use for vector decoding.
+parquet = { version = "58", default-features = false, features = ["arrow", "snap", "zstd", "async", "object_store"] }
+arrow = { version = "58", default-features = false, features = ["prettyprint"] }
+
+# Async runtime — only used inside the blocking bridge wrapping
+# object_store / parquet's async APIs. Single-threaded current-thread
+# runtime keeps the dep tree light.
+tokio = { version = "1.39", default-features = false, features = ["rt", "macros"] }
+
+# Streaming + futures glue for async parquet reads.
+futures = "0.3"
+bytes = "1"
+
+# Errors. anyhow at the boundary; structured errors map back to
+# `RuLakeError::Backend { backend, detail }` in the trait impl.
+anyhow = "1"
+
+# Logging.
+tracing = "0.1"
+
+[dev-dependencies]
+tempfile = "3"
+tokio = { version = "1.39", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros"] }
+
+[profile.release]
+opt-level = 3
+lto = "thin"
+codegen-units = 1
+strip = "symbols"

+# `ruvector-rulake-gcs` — Parquet-on-GCS BackendAdapter
+
+Implements the first cloud backend per [ADR-155 §M2](../docs/adrs/ADR-155-rulake-datalake-layer.md):
+read vectors from Parquet files on Google Cloud Storage, with cache
+coherence riding GCS's per-object generation token.
+
+Sibling Cargo package per [ADR-001](../docs/adrs/ADR-001-standalone-repo-strategy.md).
+
+## What v0.1 ships
+
+- `GcsParquetBackend` — `BackendAdapter` trait impl over GCS+Parquet.
+- **Cache coherence via GCS object generation** — every reupload bumps
+  the generation; ruLake's witness picks up the change automatically
+  through the existing `Generation::Num` variant.
+- **Cheap `current_bundle()` override** — does a HEAD on the GCS object
+  + a Parquet-footer-only schema read instead of the default impl's
+  full `pull_vectors`. ADR-004's `rulake://bundle/...` resource
+  contract requires this for any backend that backs the resource.
+- **Application Default Credentials** — auth is whatever
+  `gcloud auth application-default login` (or
+  `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=...`) sets up. No bespoke auth code.
+
+## Parquet schema contract (v0.1)
+
+Two required columns:
+
+| Column   | Type                                    | Notes |
+|----------|-----------------------------------------|-------|
+| `id`     | `INT64` (non-null)                      | Cast to `u64` by bit pattern. |
+| `vector` | `LIST<FLOAT32>` or `FixedSizeList<FLOAT32, N>` (non-null) | One row per vector; the list length is the collection's `dim`. |
+
+```sql
+-- BigQuery / DuckDB friendly DDL:
+CREATE TABLE docs (
+  id      INT64    NOT NULL,
+  vector  ARRAY<FLOAT64>     -- cast down to FLOAT32 on write
+);
+```
+
+v0.2 will add: column-name overrides via config, `LIST<DOUBLE>` with
+f64→f32 down-cast, partitioned-table fan-out, multi-file collections.
+
+## Usage
+
+```rust
+use std::sync::Arc;
+use ruvector_rulake::{cache::Consistency, RuLake, BackendAdapter};
+use ruvector_rulake_gcs::{GcsParquetBackend, GcsParquetCollection};
+
+let backend = GcsParquetBackend::open_gcs("gcs-prod", "my-vector-bucket")?;
+backend.register(GcsParquetCollection {
+    name:    "docs".into(),
+    object:  "embeddings/2026-04/docs.parquet".into(),
+    dim:     None,    // None → read from Parquet schema on first pull
+});
+
+let lake = RuLake::new(20, 42)
+    .with_consistency(Consistency::Eventual { ttl_ms: 5_000 });
+let dyn_be: Arc<dyn BackendAdapter> = Arc::new(backend);
+lake.register_backend(dyn_be)?;
+
+// First call: HEAD + footer + body fetch + RaBitQ prime. ~hundreds of ms.
+// Subsequent calls within ttl_ms: cache hit, ~1 ms.
+let hits = lake.search_one("gcs-prod", "docs", &query, 10)?;
+```
+
+## Build + test
+
+```bash
+git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/ruvnet/RuLake
+cd RuLake/gcs-backend
+cargo build --release
+cargo test --release          # 4 offline tests against in-memory ObjectStore
+
+# Live test against a real bucket (needs gcloud ADC):
+gcloud auth application-default login   # one-time

+//! ruLake GCS backend — Parquet-on-GCS `BackendAdapter` (ADR-155 §M2).
+//!
+//! # What v0.1 ships
+//!
+//! - `GcsParquetBackend` — implements `BackendAdapter` against
+//!   Parquet files on GCS (`gs://bucket/path/to/file.parquet`).
+//! - **Generation = GCS object generation number.** Every
+//!   `gsutil cp` / `gcloud storage cp` of a Parquet file bumps the
+//!   generation; ruLake's witness picks up the change automatically
+//!   via the existing `Generation::Num` variant in
+//!   `src/bundle.rs:55`.
+//! - **Cheap `current_bundle()` override** — the default impl in
+//!   `src/backend.rs:131` does a full `pull_vectors` to learn the
+//!   `dim`. That's a network read of the entire Parquet file just to
+//!   compute the witness. We override to do a HEAD on the GCS object
+//!   (~1 RTT, no body) plus a Parquet-footer-only read (a few KiB)
+//!   to learn the schema, then synthesize the bundle. ADR-004's
+//!   `rulake://bundle/{backend}/{collection}` resource explicitly
+//!   forbids the default-impl behaviour.
+//!
+//! # Parquet schema contract (v0.1)
+//!
+//! Two required columns, with operator-overridable names:
+//!
+//! - `id` — non-null `INT64` (interpreted as `u64` — bit pattern
+//!   preserved so signed input round-trips through `as u64`).
+//! - `vector` — non-null `LIST<FLOAT>` (a list of `f32`s, fixed
+//!   length per row; the length is the collection's `dim`).
+//!
+//! ```sql
+//! -- BigQuery / DuckDB friendly DDL:
+//! id      INT64    NOT NULL,
+//! vector  ARRAY<FLOAT64>   -- cast down to FLOAT (FLOAT32) at write time
+//! ```
+//!
+//! v0.2 will add: column-name overrides via config, `LIST<DOUBLE>`
+//! support with f64→f32 down-cast, partitioned-table fan-out.
+//!
+//! # Auth
+//!
+//! Application Default Credentials, via `gcloud auth application-default
+//! login` or service-account JSON in `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS`.
+//! The `object_store` crate handles ADC resolution; we don't ship our
+//! own auth code.
+
+pub mod backend;
+
+pub use backend::{GcsParquetBackend, GcsParquetCollection};
